contract.source_boundary: q, kv, grad_output, output, lse, compressed_indices
contract.kept_fast_path: selected-slot query-major QK -> P -> scaled_dP -> dQ
contract.grouping: exact forward-known compressed-group id sidecar; later token rows in the same token/window tile fold into earlier matching group rows before final global dKV atomic_add

latency_gate.read: Use the timing artifact for latency. The correctness artifact intentionally ran with no warmup and is retained only for parity because its dKV-only submode timings include no-warmup/JIT outliers.

same_kernel_dkv_submodes_from_timing.read: Even the dKV-only same-kernel sidecar schedule does not beat the retained full boundary once it is added to the fast no-dKV path.

conclusion.summary: The multi-token group-aware dKV epilogue is mathematically valid, but this Triton token4 same-kernel implementation loses the fast producer and is rejected for promotion.
why_it_did_not_materialize: The retained fast Triton baseline is effectively single-token and keeps very low live state.
Token4 grouped epilogue reduces some final dKV atomic pressure, but adds dense N-by-N route folding, additional live accumulators, and heavier producer state.
The full boundary lands at 25.84 ms median versus the 11.13 ms retained gate, so the added epilogue work costs more than the atomic savings.
conclusion.next_contract: Keep the selected-slot query-major producer, but implement a lighter epilogue in a lower-level HIP/CK/rocWMMA style or a segmented-reduction topology that avoids dense N-by-N folding and large Triton live-state growth.
